_about: L2 (clang header-AST) finalization of the oneAPI scan, run after PR #444 (clang L2 unblocked on GNU hosts) merged to main. Re-runs the three minor pairs whose binary-tier (s0/L0-L1) verdict was a documented scope divergence, now under public-header scope. Reproduce: abicheck compare <old.so> <new.so> --old-header H --new-header H --old-include I --new-include I --ast-frontend clang --format json. Headers from conda-forge dal-include / oneDNN runtime pkg / tbb-devel.

pairs.interpretation: Consistent across tiers; confirms the L2 pipeline end-to-end. tbb.h is a pure-#include umbrella header -> exercised the #444 C->C++ retry.

pairs.l2_real_breaking: serialization_tag_changed: dnnl::memory::format_tag::format_tag_last shifted (3.12 added new format tags -> the enum sentinel value changed).
pairs.interpretation: L2 refines the binary verdict: the 5 leaked symbols are libstdc++ template/shared_ptr-deleter instantiations (severity risk, non-breaking), not oneDNN API. The verdict stays BREAKING but now rests on ONE concrete public-enum change rather than opaque internal removals. dnnl.hpp has inline namespace/enum, so it was detected as C++ directly (no retry needed).

Textbook scope-divergence demotion: binary-strict BREAKING (212 removed bundled BLAS/LAPACK exports: DGETRF/SGETRF/...) -> L2 public-header scope NO_CHANGE. The full public DAAL API (25,595 functions) is byte-identical between 2025.0 and 2025.1, confirming the removed symbols are bundled-MKL internals outside oneDAL's public surface. daal.h is a pure-#include umbrella -> exercised the #444 C->C++ retry.
